---
title: "Get Started"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Get Started}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

`ggtypst` brings Typst-powered text and math rendering to `ggplot2`.

This article will be the main onboarding guide for the package. The initial
structure is in place now, and the next iteration can expand each section with
more examples and troubleshooting notes.

## Installation

```r
pak::pak("Yousa-Mirage/ggtypst")
```

## Three workflows

- `annotate_typst()` for one-off plot annotations
- `geom_typst()` for data-driven Typst labels
- `element_typst()` for Typst-rendered theme text

## Native Typst math and MiTeX math

`ggtypst` supports both Typst-native math helpers and MiTeX-backed conversion
from LaTeX-style math input.

## Next steps

For now, use the homepage and reference index as the primary documentation.
This article is scaffolded and ready for a fuller walkthrough in the next pass.
